LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER'S HEARING FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 7, 2019. THE POLICE DEPARTMENT FILED AN OPPOSITION ON MAY 9 STATING, AMONG OTHER THINGS, THAT IT RELEASED REDACTED VERSIONS OF THE DOCUMENTS SINGER SOUGHT ON MARCH 29, AND SINGER'S PETITION IS THEREFORE MOOT. SINGER SHALL FILE ANY RESPONSE TO THE CITY'S OPPOSITION ON OR BEFORE JUNE 7, 2019. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Hearing For Writ Of Mandate.

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Continued To Jul-19-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Singer, 415-278-1843 Petitioner in Pro Per.
Aileen McGrath, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4700, of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant San Francisco Police Department.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court hands out copies of the tentative ruling to parties and passes the matter. At 9:40 am, the matter is recalled. The Court asks questions re: factual proceeding background, legal analysis, mootness, victim issue, cost. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Hearing For Writ Of Mandate.

Continued to July 19, 2019 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order re: Writ Petition is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Continued To Jun-07-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Singer, Telephone 415-778-1843, appearing on his own behalf as Pro Per Plaintiff;
Aileen M. McGrath, Office of the City Attorney CCSF, Telephone 415-554-4691, appearing for Defendant;

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Hearing For Writ Of Mandate

The hearing on Anthony Singer's petition for a writ of mandate is CONTINUED to June 7, 2019. The police department filed an opposition on May 9 stating, among other things, that it released redacted versions of the documents Singer sought on March 29, and Singer's petition is therefore moot. Singer shall file any response to the City's opposition on or before June 7, 2019.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
